---
title: "Projects and Environments"
description: "Organize code into files, install dependencies safely, and run programs as small projects."
execute:
  echo: true
categories: [projects-and-environments]
order: 310
---

## Why this chapter matters

Organize code into files, install dependencies safely, and run programs as small projects.

Lessons are now grouped by coherent learner topics. A page may contain multiple
short quizzes when the page has multiple sections, so students can check one
idea before moving to the next.

::: {.callout-note title="Guiding questions"}
- Which lessons belong in this chapter?
- What should I review before moving on?
- How do the lesson sections build one practical skill?
:::

## Lesson path

- [Modules and Packages](modules-and-packages.qmd) — Organize reusable code into Python files and package folders.
- [Environments and Dependencies](environments-and-dependencies.qmd) — Use virtual environments, install packages, and understand pyproject.toml.
- [Command-Line Programs](command-line-programs.qmd) — Run scripts and accept command-line arguments with argparse.
- [Project Layout](project-layout.qmd) — Arrange source code, tests, notebooks, and documentation in predictable folders.

## Chapter checkpoint

::: {.quiz-box}
Use this short checkpoint before starting the chapter.
:::

<script type="application/json" class="fcpython-ojs-quiz-config">
{
  "id": "projects-and-environments-overview",
  "title": "Projects and Environments overview checkpoint",
  "instructions": "Choose an answer, then check your work.",
  "questions": [
    {
      "id": "chapter-purpose",
      "prompt": "What is the purpose of the Projects and Environments chapter?",
      "options": [
        "Organize code into files, install dependencies safely, and run programs as small projects.",
        "To memorize disconnected commands",
        "To skip practice",
        "To avoid debugging"
      ],
      "answer_index": 0,
      "explanation": "Organize code into files, install dependencies safely, and run programs as small projects."
    },
    {
      "id": "study-flow",
      "prompt": "Why can a lesson contain multiple quizzes?",
      "options": [
        "Each quiz checks one section before the next section builds on it.",
        "Quizzes replace examples.",
        "Multiple quizzes mean the topic is unrelated.",
        "Quizzes should only appear at the end of the course."
      ],
      "answer_index": 0,
      "explanation": "Section-level quizzes help learners review while the idea is still fresh."
    }
  ]
}
</script>

{{< include ../_includes/ojs-quiz.qmd >}}
